content.full_text:
May 6, 1992

Ms. Jeanette Turvill
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95812-0807

Dear Ms. Turvill:

Pursuant to our recent telephone conversation, please consider this letter a request for your opinion as to whether financial disclosure is required of grand jurors who are summoned only to hear criminal cases to decide whether probable cause exists to return an indictment.

For a variety of reasons, including assuring that the grand jury process complies with constitutional requirements, Nevada County has created a separate and distinct grand jury to hear criminal cases. The members of the criminal grand jury are summoned at random from the county the same way regular jurors are. The length of their service will be one year. They conduct no investigations, write no reports, and receive no responses. They have no impact on governmental decisions.

Thank you very much for your consideration of this request. If there are questions we can answer, please don't hesitate to call.

Sincerely,

CARL F. BRYAN, II
Judge of the Superior Court
